# terrible_community_general_consul_agent_check (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `args` (String) Specifies command arguments to run to update the status of the check. Requires `interval` to be provided. Mutually exclusive with `ttl`, `tcp` and `http`.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `http` (String) Checks can be registered with an HTTP endpoint. This means that Consul checks that the HTTP endpoint returns a successful HTTP status. Requires `interval` to be provided. Mutually exclusive with `args`, `ttl` and `tcp`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `interval` (String) The interval at which the service check is run. This is a number with a `s` or `m` suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example `15s` or `1m`. If no suffix is supplied `s` is used by default, for example `10` is equivalent to `10s`. Required if one of the parameters `args`, `http`, or `tcp` is specified.
- `name` (String) Required name for the service check.
- `notes` (String) Notes to attach to check when registering it.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `service_id` (String) The ID for the service, must be unique per node. If `state=absent`, defaults to the service name if supplied.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Whether the check should be present or absent.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tcp` (String) Checks can be registered with a TCP port. This means that Consul will check if the connection attempt to that port is successful (that is, the port is currently accepting connections). The format is `host:port`, for example `localhost:80`. Requires `interval` to be provided. Mutually exclusive with `args`, `ttl` and `http`.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `ttl` (String) Checks can be registered with a TTL instead of a `args` and `interval` this means that the service checks in with the agent before the TTL expires. If it does not the check is considered failed. Required if registering a check and the script an interval are missing Similar to the interval this is a number with a `s` or `m` suffix to signify the units of seconds or minutes, for example `15s` or `1m`. If no suffix is supplied `s` is used by default, for example `10` is equivalent to `10s`. Mutually exclusive with `args`, `tcp` and `http`.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `check` (String) The check as returned by the Consul HTTP API.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `operation` (String) The operation performed.
